summary: The #147/#145 genevieve pain is genuinely fixed end-to-end at BOTH gates by a single shared normalizeImpactedDomains helper (internal/validate/ownership_resolve.go) consumed by divergence.go (~L142, replacing candidateDomains := meta.Domains) and plan.go ValidatePlan (~L130, normalizing `impacted` before feeding checkADRCoverage + checkADRCitations). Trace 1 (divergence): a file-path entry like internal/genevieve/review.py is glob-matched against every domain's OWNERSHIP paths:, resolves to the genevieve domain NAME, the changed file attributes to genevieve, is covered by cited Accepted ADR-0099 -> coveredAccepted silent pass, zero adr-divergence-unowned. Trace 2 (plan): the SAME normalized {genevieve} feeds both plan gates -> no adr-coverage-missing, no adr-cite-irrelevant. The named-domain path is preserved (Rule 1 keeps a dir-name verbatim), the blast-radius guard is intact (a genuinely-unowned CHANGED file still fails adr-divergence-unowned; TestCompleteRejectsUndeclaredDomainTouch still passes), and zero/ambiguous-owner entries now hard-error with a clear impacted-domains-resolve message instead of silently misclassifying. E2E tests are faithful reproductions of the genevieve case (file-path Impacted Domains + genevieve OWNERSHIP paths: internal/genevieve/** + Accepted ADR declaring genevieve + changed file in that domain), not weaker proxies. go build ./... succeeds and all 12 targeted tests (4 divergence + 5 plan + normalize unit + 2 blast-radius regressions) pass. The user no longer needs --override-adr for this case: the gate now resolves the owner and provides value instead of friction; a real unowned file still gets caught, so the gate is correctly scoped rather than disabled.
